content.text:
<recommended_plugins>
Here is a list of plugins that are available but not installed. If the user's query would benefit from one of these plugins, use the `request_plugin_install` tool to suggest that they install it. Pass the parenthesized ID as `plugin_id`. For example, suggest the Google Drive plugin if the query could possibly be better answered with access to Google Drive.

- Atlassian Rovo (atlassian-rovo@openai-curated-remote)
- Box (box@openai-curated-remote)
- Figma (figma@openai-curated-remote)
- Gmail (gmail@openai-curated-remote)
- Google Calendar (google-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Google Drive (google-drive@openai-curated-remote)
- Notion (notion@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Calendar (outlook-calendar@openai-curated-remote)
- Outlook Email (outlook-email@openai-curated-remote)
- SharePoint (sharepoint@openai-curated-remote)
- Slack (slack@openai-curated-remote)
- Teams (teams@openai-curated-remote)
</recommended_plugins>
I’m following the security discovery workflow and will inspect only the assigned diff paths plus direct auth/policy support if the diff requires it.

input.input:
const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"scan_dir=/var/folders/hs/pt2n3nn16k3935lw_ljnl5t40000gn/T/codex-security-scans/cyspbot/eb7e37e9240c385b9061f0f4a59a91dd8f68d287_20260710T000000Z
plugin_dir=/Users/cysp/.codex/plugins/cache/openai-curated-remote/codex-security/0.1.11
for f in "$scan_dir/artifacts/01_context/security_guidance.md" "$scan_dir/artifacts/01_context/threat_model.md" "$plugin_dir/skills/references/security-guidance.md" "$plugin_dir/skills/security-scan/references/scan-artifacts-and-ledger.md"; do
  printf '\n===== %s =====\n' "$f"
  sed -n '1,260p' "$f"
done","workdir":"/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":40000});
text(r.output);

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"scan_dir=/var/folders/hs/pt2n3nn16k3935lw_ljnl5t40000gn/T/codex-security-scans/cyspbot/eb7e37e9240c385b9061f0f4a59a91dd8f68d287_20260710T000000Z
base=99475317089cc75f1451b1893ea31ccd17284a92
head=eb7e37e9240c385b9061f0f4a59a91dd8f68d287
paths=(
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/token-policy.ts
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/trusted-oidc-issuers.ts
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/token-exchange.ts
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/worker.ts
)
printf '%s\n' '## Line counts'
wc -l "${paths[@]}"
printf '%s\n' '## Assigned diffs'
git diff --find-renames "$base...$head" -- "${paths[@]}"
for p in "${paths[@]}"; do
  printf '\n## FULL FILE: %s\n' "$p"
  nl -ba "$p"
done
printf '%s\n' '## Existing relevant ledger rows'
if [ -f "$scan_dir/artifacts/02_discovery/work_ledger.jsonl" ]; then
  rg -n 'workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/(policy/token-policy|policy/trusted-oidc-issuers|token-exchange|worker)\.ts' "$scan_dir/artifacts/02_discovery/work_ledger.jsonl" || true
fi","workdir":"/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":60000});
text(r.output);

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"p=workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/token-policy.ts
printf '%s\n' '## token-policy.ts lines 1-220'
nl -ba "$p" | sed -n '1,220p'
printf '%s\n' '## token-policy.ts lines 221-440'
nl -ba "$p" | sed -n '221,440p'
printf '%s\n' '## token-policy.ts lines 441-595'
nl -ba "$p" | sed -n '441,595p'","workdir":"/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":50000});
text(r.output);

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"base=99475317089cc75f1451b1893ea31ccd17284a92
head=eb7e37e9240c385b9061f0f4a59a91dd8f68d287
for p in \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/trusted-oidc-issuers.ts \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/token-exchange.ts \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/worker.ts; do
  printf '\n## DIFF: %s\n' "$p"
  git diff --find-renames "$base...$head" -- "$p"
done","workdir":"/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":40000});
text(r.output);

content.text: The changed route now delegates authentication and issuance through a per-request runtime, so I’m tracing that narrow adapter chain to confirm which verified claims and policy rules reach the token-minting sink.

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"scan_dir=/var/folders/hs/pt2n3nn16k3935lw_ljnl5t40000gn/T/codex-security-scans/cyspbot/eb7e37e9240c385b9061f0f4a59a91dd8f68d287_20260710T000000Z

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"scan_dir=/var/folders/hs/pt2n3nn16k3935lw_ljnl5t40000gn/T/codex-security-scans/cyspbot/eb7e37e9240c385b9061f0f4a59a91dd8f68d287_20260710T000000Z

for p in \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/token-policy.ts \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/trusted-oidc-issuers.ts \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/token-exchange.ts \
  workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/worker.ts; do
  count=$(rg -F -c "\"path\":\"$p\"" "$ledger" || true)
  printf '%s %s\n' "$count" "$p"
done
printf '%s\n' '## Receipts'
rg -n '"path":"workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/(policy/token-policy|policy/trusted-oidc-issuers|token-exchange|worker)\.ts"' "$ledger"
printf '%s\n' '## Candidate artifact state'
if [ -e "$scan_dir/artifacts/02_discovery/raw_candidates.jsonl" ]; then
  wc -l "$scan_dir/artifacts/02_discovery/raw_candidates.jsonl"
else
  printf '%s\n' 'raw_candidates.jsonl absent (no candidate emitted by this worker)'
fi","workdir":"/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":20000});
text(r.output);

content.text:
Reviewed all assigned diff rows and appended one completion receipt each to the discovery ledger:

- `policy/token-policy.ts`
- `policy/trusted-oidc-issuers.ts`
- `token-exchange.ts`
- `worker.ts`

No plausible security candidates found. Direct auth, issuer-adapter, policy-rule, and issuance support confirms issuance remains gated by fixed issuer verification, exact audience/resource/permission checks, and a CEL condition that must evaluate to `true`.
